boundary_probes_documented_not_a_finding.why_not_a_finding: These are OUTSIDE the caseless-LABEL gluing class and match the fix's explicitly-documented, deliberate boundary: (a) 東京Manager/TokyoManager glue a ROLE word (not one of the narrow _ARTIFACT_LABEL_WORDS) — the label-vs-role asymmetry the commit calls out, and it applies EQUALLY to Latin cased nouns (TokyoManager leaks identically); (b) 東京Zürich glues two proper nouns with NO label word at all; (c) 東京Salaries uses the plural 'Salaries' which is intentionally exact-match-safe (commit: 'plural/possessive forms ... are exact-match-safe and unaffected'). All three are pre-existing, deliberate tradeoffs mirrored for Latin — NOT caseless-specific regressions, NOT loop-blocking.

new_findings.class: JD keyword tokenizer (_WORD_RE) complex-script word-boundary fragmentation — DISTINCT from the CamelCase-gluing class (NF-final-pass-001/002); a pre-existing issue surfaced by this closure sweep
new_findings.epistemic: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] leak observed on prod @54c28e5 (insights API + real-browser panel, twice + post-restart); [INFERRED] root cause from deployed-source reading + local tokenization probe
new_findings.summary: The JD Keyword Coverage panel leaks garbage fragment chips for CJK-adjacent COMBINING-MARK scripts (Thai, Devanagari, ...). _WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[^\W_](?:[^\W_]|[+#./-])*") treats Thai/Devanagari combining vowel-signs/anusvara (e.g. U+0E38 ◌ุ, U+0E47 ◌็, U+0E31 ◌ั, U+0902 ◌ं) as non-word chars, so it SPLITS a single Thai/Devanagari token at every combining mark. A standalone Thai city name — with NO gluing and NO label word — therefore fragments into meaningless sub-tokens, and any fragment >=3 chars survives _is_semantic_keyword and surfaces as a top chip: 'กรุงเทพ' (Bangkok) -> ['กร','งเทพ'] -> chip 'งเทพ'; 'เชียงใหม่' (Chiang Mai) -> chip 'ยงใหม'. This is NOT the CamelCase-gluing class and is NOT introduced by the NF-final-pass-002 fix — the fix correctly drops the label-bearing half of a gluing ('Salaryกรุงเทพ' -> ['Salaryกร','งเทพ'], the 'Salaryกร' hump ['Salary'] is now flagged and dropped); what leaks is the orphaned non-label script fragment.
reproduction: 1. Log in as a Pro user with a résumé.
2. Create/seed a Job whose description contains a STANDALONE (unglued, no label) Thai city name — e.g. 'Offices in กรุงเทพ and เชียงใหม่ and ภูเก็ต cities.' plus a couple legit tech terms.
3. Attach a cover letter for that job.
4. GET /cover-letters/{id}/insights -> keywords.items CONTAINS the garbage fragments 'งเทพ' and 'ยงใหม' as chips (evidence api/probe-thai-standalone.json).
5. Open in Cover Letter Studio -> the JD Keyword Coverage panel renders those Thai fragments (screenshots/panel-C1-thai-fragment.png).
new_findings.expected: A complex-script proper noun in a JD is either surfaced whole as a keyword or filtered — never fragmented into meaningless sub-tokens that appear as chips on the paid rail.
new_findings.observed: Standalone 'กรุงเทพ' -> 'งเทพ' and 'เชียงใหม่' -> 'ยงใหม' leaked as chips on prod @54c28e5 (job C1, no gluing at all). Also visible via the gluing path: job B1 'Salaryกรุงเทพ' -> 'งเทพ' chip (the 'Salary' half correctly dropped by the fix). Reproduced twice and post-restart (pass2/pass3/probe), identical. Devanagari standalone 'मुंबई' -> ['म','बई'] fragments too but the 'बई' fragment is <3 chars and is dropped by min-length, so Devanagari standalone happens not to leak (its leak surfaces only when a label is glued, which the fix now catches).
new_findings.root_cause_inferred: apps/api/app/routers/cover_letters.py::_WORD_RE — the character class [^\W_] excludes Unicode combining marks (general category Mn/Mc), which Thai and Devanagari use extensively as inline vowel signs. The regex therefore breaks a single orthographic word at each combining mark instead of keeping the grapheme cluster together. _is_semantic_keyword's gibberish guard only rejects tokens of len>=6 with no ASCII [aeiouy] vowel — a 3-5 char caseless fragment sails through. (Cyrillic/Greek standalone words of len>=6 are instead caught by that same ASCII-vowel gibberish guard, e.g. standalone Москва was dropped — so this specifically bites 3-5 char combining-mark fragments.)
new_findings.fix_hint: Any of: (a) include Unicode combining marks in the word class — e.g. _WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[^\W_][\w\u0300-\u036f\u0e00-\u0e7f\u0900-\u097f...+#./-]*", re.U) or switch to a grapheme-cluster / \X-aware tokenizer (the `regex` module's \X, or unicodedata normalization + combining-mark reattachment); or (b) after tokenizing, reject a token that is (or begins with) an orphaned combining mark or is a sub-grapheme fragment; or (c) extend the gibberish guard to reject short pure-caseless-script fragments that are not whole words. Materiality is LOW so a targeted _WORD_RE widening is likely sufficient.
new_findings.materiality: LOW: a meaningless script fragment on the paid JD-Keyword-Coverage rail — no PII, no security, no fabricated user content, and the CamelCase-gluing class (the reported finding) is genuinely closed. Trigger surface is any JD mentioning a Thai/Devanagari (and likely other combining-mark script) place/proper name, glued OR standalone — international boards emit these. Filed as loop-exit input and to keep the tokenizer honest; NOT a regression and NOT a reopening of NF-final-pass-002.
